content.problemStatement:
A certain type of steel is graded according to the following conditions.


**Hardness** of the steel must be greater than 50
**Carbon content** of the steel must be less than 0.7
**Tensile strength** must be greater than 5600
The grades awarded are as follows:


Grade is 10 if all three conditions are met
Grade is 9 if conditions (1) and (2) are met
Grade is 8 if conditions (2) and (3) are met
Grade is 7 if conditions (1) and (3) are met
Grade is 6 if only one condition is met
Grade is 5 if none of the three conditions are met
Write a program to display the grade of the steel, based on the values of hardness, carbon content and tensile strength of the steel, given by the user.


Input Format
The first line contains an integer **T**, total number of testcases. Then follow **T** lines, each line contains three numbers **hardness**, **carbon content** and **tensile strength** of the steel.



Output Format
For each test case, print Grade of the steel depending on Conditions, in a new line.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three numbers: hardness (integer), carbon content (real number), and tensile strength (integer), separated by spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer representing the grade of the steel on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1 meets all three conditions → grade 10. Test case 2 meets only the hardness condition (hardness>50) → grade 6. Test case 3 meets none → grade 5.
First line satisfies all three conditions → grade 10. Second line satisfies carbon<0.7 and tensile>5600 (conditions 2 and 3) → grade 8.
All three values are exactly on the boundary, none of the strict inequalities hold, so no condition is satisfied → grade 5.

solution.approach: For each test case we evaluate the three independent conditions: (1) hardness > 50, (2) carbon content < 0.7, (3) tensile strength > 5600. Using three boolean variables we first check if all three are true (grade 10). If not, we check the three possible pairs in the order required by the statement (grade 9, 8, 7). If none of the pairs match but at least one condition is true we output grade 6; otherwise grade 5. The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.
